name: Anxiety
description: Persistent worry about the future or about things outside your control.

responses.angle: Divide the world into what you control and what you do not. Worry only about the first.
Anxiety is the mind grasping at futures that do not yet exist. Return to the breath.
Most of what we fear will never happen. Tranquility is built from modest, present pleasures.

name: Lack of meaning
description: A pervasive sense that nothing you do really matters.

responses.angle: Meaning is not found, it is made. You are condemned to author it.
Stop demanding meaning from the universe; revolt by living fully anyway.
Meaning shows up in committed action, not in argument about meaning.
Purpose is plentiful where suffering exists to be reduced.

name: Fear of death
description: Dread about your own mortality, or about losing those you love.

responses.angle: Death is a natural process, not a misfortune. Remember it daily to live better.
Where death is, you are not. Suffering needs a subject.
The self that fears death is itself the illusion you can see through.
Mortality is what makes any single choice weigh something.

name: Loneliness
description: Feeling unseen, disconnected, or solitary even among others.

responses.angle: You belong to a cosmopolis of reasoning beings. Solitude is not loneliness if you are good company to yourself.
Friendship is the greatest of life's sustainable pleasures. Cultivate a small garden of it.
Loneliness softens when you see how interconnected all things already are.

name: Burnout
description: Exhaustion from prolonged effort, often without proportional reward.

responses.angle: Wu wei — effortless action. You are pushing where you should be flowing.
If the boulder rolls back down, choose how you walk back to it. Sisyphus can be happy.
The wealth nature asks for is small. Recalibrate what you are chasing.

name: Identity crisis
description: Uncertainty about who you are, what you value, or where you belong.

responses.angle: There is no essence underneath. You build identity through choices you stand by.
There is no fixed self to be in crisis. Investigate the assumption itself.
Identity is what your actions add up to. Try things; iterate.

name: Attachment
description: Suffering caused by clinging to people, outcomes, or self-images.

responses.angle: Attachment is the root of suffering. Loving without grasping is possible.
Treat what you love as borrowed, not owned. Gratitude replaces clinging.
Possessions own you. Live light; need almost nothing.

name: Social alienation
description: Feeling out of step with the values or rhythms of those around you.

responses.angle: The crowd's values are often unexamined. Standing apart can be a sign of clear sight.
Return to nature, to simplicity. Some friction is just the world being noisy.
Alienation is honest. The bad faith would be pretending to fit.

name: Overthinking
description: Looping mental analysis that crowds out action and rest.

responses.angle: Ask only: is this in my control, and is action required? If not, set it down.
Thought is one of the senses. You do not have to follow every signal.
Truth shows up in trying. Stop deliberating; run the experiment.

name: Lack of discipline
description: Knowing what would be good for you and not doing it.

responses.angle: Virtue is a daily practice, not a temperament. Begin again, smaller, today.
Strip life back. Comfort is often what is rotting your resolve.

name: Emotional suffering
description: Sadness, grief, shame, or other intense feeling that is hard to bear.

responses.angle: The pain is real; the story you tell about it is optional. Examine the story.
Sit with it as you would with weather. It is already changing.
Feeling is not weakness. It is part of taking your existence seriously.

name: Existential dread
description: A confrontation with meaninglessness, infinity, or the indifference of the universe.

responses.angle: Dread comes from demanding what the universe cannot give. Revolt by living.
Dread is the first taste of freedom. Stay with it; then choose.
Look directly at the void. It is more bearable than the lie of obligation.
Let go of the demand for permanence. The Tao is already carrying you.
